essential_concepts.name: Markov Decision Process (MDP)
essential_concepts.description: A Markov Decision Process is a mathematical model used to describe a situation where an agent can take actions in an uncertain environment.
mastery_criteria: Student can define an MDP and its components.
Students should be able to understand the concept of MDP and its applications.
common_misconceptions: Students often think that MDPs are only used for decision-making in complex situations.

essential_concepts.name: Dynamic Programming (DP)
essential_concepts.description: Dynamic programming is a method used to solve optimization problems by breaking them down into smaller sub-problems.
mastery_criteria: Student can explain the concept of dynamic programming and its applications.
common_misconceptions: Students often think that DP is only used for solving linear programming problems.

essential_concepts.name: Bellman Equations
essential_concepts.description: The Bellman equations are a set of equations used to solve dynamic programming problems.
mastery_criteria: Student can write the Bellman equations and understand their significance.
common_misconceptions: Students often think that the Bellman equations are only used for solving simple optimization problems.
